last_task.subject: I've been thinking a lot about how we share tasks. A to-do list or kandban board is a simple engineering task for CS100-level students. We have Bun and SQL and everything is a task. Now we have multiple agents. One has multiple wallets/identities. One has a different dispatch engine. The human watches via the observatory and individual dashboards. What's the SpaceX engineering 5-step answer to how we all collaborate efficiently and effectively on completing tasks toward a shared goal? Originally github was one idea but since Spark got banned and others aren't setup we do have an Umbrel VM available and could try a github alternative that's local and simple for the team. I also want to focus on having the LLM do what the LLM is best at, and to use scripts, skills, sensors, and dispatch patterns as helpers to get things done efficiently. That's why we built ourselves up this way, now as we evolve we have to hold ourselves to those same standards.
